001package guiMultipleRoleDispatch;
002
003/*******
004 * <p> Title: ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch Class. </p>
005 * 
006 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based Multiple Role Dispatch Page.  This class provides the 
007 * controller actions to allow the user to select which role to use for this particular user. 
008 * If the user has only one authorized role, this page is not used.  Only when a specific user
009 * has been authorized to play more than one role, that user must select which role to play
010 * after logging in.  The fact that this user has multiple authorized roles is not made visible
011 * before the user logs in.  We believe the system should provide no information until that user
012 * has logged in.
013 * 
014 * The class has been written assuming that the View or the Model are the only class methods that
015 * can invoke these methods.  This is why each has been declared at "protected".  Do not change any
016 * of these methods to public.</p>
017 * 
018 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
019 * 
020 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
021 * 
022 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
023 * @version 1.01                2025-09-16 Update Javadoc documentation
024 *  
025 */
026
027public class ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch {
028
029        /*-********************************************************************************************
030
031        User Interface Actions for this page
032        
033        This controller is not a class that gets instantiated.  Rather, it is a collection of protected
034        static methods that can be called by the View (which is a singleton instantiated object) and 
035        the Model is often just a stub, or will be a singleton instantiated object.
036
037         */
038        
039        /**
040         * Default constructor is not used.
041         */
042        public ControllerMultipleRoleDispatch() {
043        }
044                
045        /**********
046         * <p> Method: performRole() </p>
047         * 
048         * <p> Description: This method directs the execution to one of the various home pages based on
049         * the current value of the SelectRole ComboBox. </p>
050         * 
051         */
052        static protected void performRole() {
053                
054                // Fetch the role from the SelectRole ComboBox
055                String role = guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.combobox_SelectRole.getValue();
056
057                // See if Admin
058                if (role.compareTo("Admin") == 0) {
059                        
060                        // It is Admin Role
061                        guiAdminHome.ViewAdminHome.displayAdminHome(
062                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theStage, 
063                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theUser);
064                        
065                // See if Student
066                } else if (role.compareTo("Student") == 0) {
067
068                        // It is Student Role
069                        guiStudentHome.ViewStudentHome.displayStudentHome(
070                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theStage, 
071                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theUser);
072                        
073                // See if Staff
074                } else if (role.compareTo("Staff") == 0) {
075                        
076                        // It is Staff Role
077                        guiStaffHome.ViewStaffHome.displayStaffHome(
078                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theStage, 
079                                        guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theUser);
080                        
081                } else {
082                        // Invalid role
083                        System.out.println("*** ERROR *** GUIMultipleRoleDispatch was asked to dispatch to " +
084                                        "a role, " + role + ", that is not supported!");
085                        System.exit(0);
086                }
087        }
088        
089        
090        /**********
091         * <p> Method: performLogout() </p>
092         * 
093         * <p> Description: This method logs out the current user and proceeds to the normal login
094         * page where existing users can log in or potential new users with a invitation code can
095         * start the process of setting up an account. </p>
096         * 
097         */
098        static protected void performLogout() {
099                guiUserLogin.ViewUserLogin.displayUserLogin(
100                                guiMultipleRoleDispatch.ViewMultipleRoleDispatch.theStage);
101        }
102        
103        
104        /**********
105         * <p> Method: performQuit() </p>
106         * 
107         * <p> Description: This method terminates the execution of the program.  It leaves the
108         * database in a state where the normal login page will be displayed when the application is
109         * restarted.</p>
110         * 
111         */
112        static protected void performQuit() {
113                System.exit(0);
114        }
115
116}
